title: Deuteronomy 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 These are the words Moses spoke to all Israel. They were in the desert east of the Jordan River. They were in the Arabah, across from Suph. The place was between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
2 It is an eleven-day trip from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of Mount Seir.
3 In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites. He told them all that the LORD had commanded for them.
4 This was after he had beaten Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon. He had also beaten Og king of Bashan at Edrei. Og lived in Ashtaroth.
5 On the east side of the Jordan, in the land of Moab, Moses began to explain this law. He said:
6 The LORD our God said to us at Horeb, "You have stayed at this mountain long enough.

7 Start your trip again. Go to the hill country of the Amorites. Go to all the nearby peoples in the Arabah, in the hills, and in the low hills. Go to the Negev and along the seacoast. Go to the land of the Canaanites and to Lebanon, as far as the great Euphrates River.
8 See, I have placed the land before you. Go in and take it as your own. The LORD promised this land to your fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. He will give it to their children after them."
9 At that time I said to you, "I cannot carry this heavy load for you alone.
10 The LORD your God has made you many. Today you are as many as the stars in the sky.
11 May the LORD, the God of your fathers, make you a thousand times more. May he bless you, as he promised.
12 But how can I carry your troubles, your loads, and your arguments by myself?
13 Choose wise, smart, and respected men from each of your tribes. I will make them your leaders."
14 And you answered me, "What you plan to do is good."
15 So I took the leaders of your tribes — wise and respected men. I made them leaders over you. They were leaders over 1,000s, 100s, 50s, and 10s. They were also officers for your tribes.
16 At that time I told your judges, "Hear the arguments between your brothers. Judge fairly between a man and his brother, or a stranger living among you.
17 Show no favoritism when you judge. Hear both small and great alike. Do not be afraid of anyone, because judgment belongs to God. Bring any case too hard for you to me, and I will hear it."
18 And at that time I told you all the things you were to do.

19 Just as the LORD our God had commanded us, we left Horeb. We went toward the hill country of the Amorites. We went through all that vast and scary desert you have seen. Then we reached Kadesh-barnea.
20 I said, "You have reached the hill country of the Amorites. The LORD our God is giving it to us.
21 See, the LORD your God has placed the land before you. Go up and take it as your own. The LORD, the God of your fathers, has told you to do this. Do not be afraid or lose heart."
22 Then all of you came to me and said, "Let us send men ahead of us to search the land. They can bring back word about the route to take and the cities to enter."
23 The plan seemed good to me. So I chose twelve men from you, one from each tribe.
24 They left and went up into the hill country. They came to the Valley of Eshcol and explored the land.
25 They took some of the fruit of the land in their hands. They carried it down to us and brought us word, "The land that the LORD our God is giving us is good."
26 But you were not willing to go up. You did not obey the command of the LORD your God.
27 You complained in your tents and said, "The LORD hates us. He brought us out of Egypt to hand us over to the Amorites. They will destroy us.
28 Where can we go? Our brothers have made our hearts weak. They said, 'The people are bigger and taller than we are. The cities are large, with walls up to the sky. We even saw the Anakim there.'"
29 So I said to you, "Do not be scared or afraid of them!
30 The LORD your God goes before you. He will fight for you, just as you saw him do for you in Egypt.
31 He fought for you in the desert too. There the LORD your God carried you. He carried you as a father carries his son, all the way to this place."
32 But even after all this, you did not trust the LORD your God.
33 He went before you on the trip. He went in fire by night and in a cloud by day. He searched for a place for you to camp. He showed you the road to take.
34 When the LORD heard your words, he grew angry. He made a strong promise and said,
35 "Not one of the men of this evil generation will see the good land. I promised this land to your fathers.
36 Only Caleb son of Jephunneh will see it. I will give him and his children after him the land he walked on. He followed the LORD with all his heart."
37 The LORD was also angry with me because of you. He said, "Not even you will enter the land.
38 Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you, will enter it. Help him to be strong. He will lead Israel to take the land.
39 The little ones you said would become captives — your children who did not yet know good from evil — they will enter the land. I will give it to them, and they will take it as their own.
40 But you must turn back. Head for the desert along the route to the Red Sea."

41 Then you replied, "We have sinned against the LORD. We will go up and fight, just as the LORD our God commanded us." So each of you put on his weapons of war. You thought it would be easy to go up into the hill country.
42 But the LORD said to me, "Tell them not to go up and fight. I am not with them. Their enemies will defeat them."
43 So I spoke to you, but you would not listen. You did not obey the command of the LORD. You went up proudly into the hill country.
44 Then the Amorites who lived in those hills came out against you. They chased you like a swarm of bees. They chased you away from Seir all the way to Hormah.
45 You came back and wept before the LORD. But he would not listen to your voice. He did not pay attention to you.
46 So you stayed in Kadesh for a long time — a very long time.

title: Deuteronomy 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Then we turned back to the desert by the Red Sea. The LORD had told me to do this. We walked around Mount Seir for many days.
2 At this time the LORD said to me,
3 "You have walked around this hill country long enough. Now turn to the north.
4 Tell the people, 'You will go through the land of your brothers, the sons of Esau. They live in Seir. They will be afraid of you, so be very careful.
5 Do not start a fight with them. I will not give you any of their land. I will not give you even a small piece of it. I have given Mount Seir to Esau as his own land.
6 You must pay them with silver for the food you eat. You must also pay for the water you drink.'"
7 The LORD your God has blessed all your work. He has watched over you on your long trip. The LORD your God has been with you for forty years. You have not missed anything.

8 So we passed by our brothers, the sons of Esau. They live in Seir. We turned away from the Arabah road. That road comes up from Elath and Ezion-geber. We traveled along the road of the Wilderness of Moab.
9 Then the LORD said to me, "Do not trouble the Moabites. Do not start a war with them. I will not give you any of their land. I have given Ar to the sons of Lot as their own land."
10 (The Emites used to live there. They were a great and large people. They were as tall as the Anakites.
11 Like the Anakites, people also counted them as Rephaim. But the Moabites called them Emites.
12 The Horites used to live in Seir. But the sons of Esau pushed them out. They destroyed the Horites and lived in their place. Israel did the same thing in the land the LORD gave them.)
13 "Now get up and cross the Brook of Zered." So we crossed the Brook of Zered.

14 We traveled from Kadesh-barnea until we crossed the Brook of Zered. This took thirty-eight years. By then all the soldiers of that generation had died from the camp. This happened just as the LORD had promised.
15 The LORD acted against them. He removed them from the camp until they had all died.
16 When all the soldiers among the people had died,
17 the LORD said to me,
18 "Today you will cross the border of Moab at Ar.
19 When you come close to the Ammonites, do not trouble them. Do not start a fight with them. I will not give you any of their land. I have given it to the sons of Lot as their own land."
20 (People counted that land too as the land of the Rephaim. They used to live there. But the Ammonites called them Zamzummites.
21 They were a great and large people. They were as tall as the Anakites. But the LORD destroyed them before the Ammonites. The Ammonites pushed them out and lived in their place.
22 The LORD did the same for the sons of Esau, who live in Seir. He destroyed the Horites before them. They pushed them out and have lived in their place to this day.
23 The Avvim lived in villages as far as Gaza. But the Caphtorites destroyed them. The Caphtorites came out of Caphtor and lived in their place.)
24 "Get up and set out. Cross the Arnon Valley. Look, I have given Sihon the Amorite into your hand. He is the king of Heshbon. I have given you his land too. Start to take it. Fight him in war.
25 Today I will begin to put fear of you on all the nations under heaven. They will hear the news about you. They will shake with great pain because of you."
26 So I sent messengers from the Wilderness of Kedemoth. They went to Sihon king of Heshbon to ask for peace. I said,
27 "Let us pass through your land. We will stay on the main road. We will not turn to the right or to the left.
28 You can sell us food to eat. You can sell us water to drink. We will pay with silver. Just let us pass through on foot.
29 The sons of Esau in Seir did this for us. The Moabites in Ar did this for us too. Let us cross the Jordan into the land the LORD our God is giving us."
30 But Sihon king of Heshbon would not let us pass through. The LORD your God had made his spirit hard and his heart firm. God did this to give him into your hand. And that is what happened this day.
31 Then the LORD said to me, "Look, I have begun to give Sihon and his land to you. Now begin to take his land."
32 So Sihon and his whole army came out to fight us at Jahaz.
33 The LORD our God gave him to us. We defeated him, his sons, and his whole army.
34 At that time we captured all his cities. We gave over to be destroyed the people of every city. We killed even the women and children. We left no one alive.
35 We took only the farm animals for ourselves. We also took the goods from the cities we captured.
36 We won from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley. We took the city in the valley too, and as far as Gilead. No city had walls too strong for us. The LORD our God gave us all of them.

37 But you did not go near the land of the Ammonites. You did not go near the banks of the Jabbok River. You did not go near the cities of the hill country. You stayed away from every place the LORD our God had told you not to enter.

title: Deuteronomy 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Then we turned and went up the road to Bashan. Og king of Bashan and his whole army came out to fight us at Edrei.
2 But the LORD said to me, "Do not fear him. I have given him to you, with all his people and his land. Do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon."
3 So the LORD our God also gave Og king of Bashan and his whole army to us. We killed them all; no one was left.
4 At that time we took all sixty of his cities. We took every city — the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
5 All these cities had high walls, gates, and strong bars. There were also many villages without walls.
6 We destroyed them completely, as we did to Sihon king of Heshbon. We killed the men, women, and children of every city.
7 But we kept the farm animals and the goods we took from the cities.
8 At that time we took the land of the two Amorite kings across the Jordan. It went from the Arnon Valley to Mount Hermon.
9 (The Sidonians call Hermon "Sirion." The Amorites call it "Senir.")
10 We took all the cities on the high flat land, all of Gilead, and all of Bashan. This went as far as Salecah and Edrei in Og's kingdom.
11 (Only Og king of Bashan was left of the Rephaim. His iron bed was about 4 meters long and 1.8 meters wide. It is still in Rabbah of the Ammonites.)
12 At that time we took this land. I gave the Reubenites and Gadites the land beyond Aroer along the Arnon Valley. I also gave them half of the hill country of Gilead and its cities.
13 I gave the half-tribe of Manasseh the rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, Og's kingdom. (People used to call the whole region of Argob the land of the Rephaim.)
14 Jair, from Manasseh's family, took the whole region of Argob. It reached the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites. He gave Bashan his own name, Havvoth-jair, which it still has today.
15 I gave Gilead to Machir.
16 I gave the Reubenites and Gadites the land from Gilead to the Arnon Valley. The middle of the valley was the border. It reached up to the Jabbok River, the border of the Ammonites.
17 The Jordan River in the Arabah was the western border. It went from Chinnereth to the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea), below the slopes of Pisgah.
18 At that time I told you, "The LORD your God has given you this land. All your strong fighting men must cross over, ready for battle, in front of your brothers, the Israelites.
19 But your wives, your children, and your animals may stay in the cities I have given you. I know you have many animals.

20 Stay until the LORD gives your brothers peace, as He has given you. They will also take the land that the LORD your God is giving them across the Jordan. Then each of you may return to the land I have given you."
21 At that time I told Joshua, "Your own eyes have seen all that the LORD your God did to these two kings. The LORD will do the same to all the kingdoms you are about to enter.
22 Do not be afraid of them. The LORD your God Himself will fight for you."
23 At that time I also begged the LORD:
24 "O Lord GOD, You have begun to show Your greatness and power to Your servant. What god in heaven or on earth can do such works and mighty acts as Yours?
25 Please let me cross over and see the good land beyond the Jordan — that pleasant hill country and Lebanon!"
26 But the LORD was angry with me because of you. He would not listen to me. "Enough," the LORD said to me. "Do not speak to Me again about this.
27 Go to the top of Pisgah. Look to the west, north, south, and east. See the land with your own eyes. You will not cross this Jordan.
28 But give Joshua his job. Encourage him and make him strong. He will cross over in front of the people. He will help them take the land that you will see."
29 So we stayed in the valley opposite Beth-peor.

title: Deuteronomy 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Hear now, O Israel, the statutes and rules I am teaching you. Follow them so that you may live. Then you will enter and take the land that the LORD, the God of your fathers, is giving you.
2 Do not add to what I command you. Do not take away from it. Keep the commandments of the LORD your God that I give you.
3 Your own eyes saw what the LORD did at Baal-peor. The LORD your God destroyed every one of you who followed Baal of Peor.
4 But you who stayed close to the LORD your God are alive today, every one of you.
5 See, I have taught you statutes and rules, just as the LORD my God commanded me. Follow them in the land you are about to enter and take.
6 Obey them carefully. This will show your wisdom and understanding to the nations. They will hear all these statutes and say, "Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people."
7 What nation is so great that it has a god as near as the LORD our God? He is near us whenever we call on Him.
8 And what nation is so great that it has rules as right as this whole law I set before you today?
9 Only be careful and watch yourselves closely. Do not forget the things your eyes have seen. Do not let them leave your heart as long as you live. Teach them to your children and grandchildren.

10 Remember the day you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb. The LORD said to me, "Gather the people before Me. Let them hear My words. Then they will learn to fear Me all the days they live on the earth. They will also teach My words to their children."
11 You came near and stood at the foot of the mountain. The mountain burned with fire up to the sky, with dark clouds and thick darkness.
12 Then the LORD spoke to you out of the fire. You heard the sound of the words. But you saw no form; there was only a voice.
13 He told you about His covenant. He commanded you to follow it — the Ten Commandments. He wrote them on two stone tablets.
14 At that time the LORD commanded me to teach you the statutes and rules. You must follow them in the land you are crossing the Jordan to take.
15 So you saw no form of any kind on the day the LORD spoke to you out of the fire at Horeb. Be very careful.
16 Do not do evil. Do not make an idol for yourselves in any shape — like a man or a woman,
17 like any animal on the earth, or any bird that flies in the air,
18 or any creature that crawls on the ground, or any fish in the waters below.

19 When you look up to the sky and see the sun, the moon, and the stars — all the stars of the sky — do not be tricked. Do not bow down to them or worship them. The LORD your God has given them to all the nations under heaven.
20 But the LORD has taken you and brought you out of the burning furnace, out of Egypt. He brought you out to be His own people, as you are today.
21 The LORD was angry with me because of you. He swore that I would not cross the Jordan. I would not enter the good land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance.
22 I will not be crossing the Jordan. I must die in this land. But you will cross over and take that good land.
23 Be careful. Do not forget the covenant of the LORD your God that He made with you. Do not make an idol for yourselves in the form of anything He has told you not to make.
24 For the LORD your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.

25 After you have children and grandchildren, you will live in the land a long time. Then you may do evil and make an idol of any form. You may do what is evil in the sight of the LORD your God and make Him angry.

26 I call the sky and the earth as witnesses against you today. You will quickly be lost from the land that you are crossing the Jordan to take. You will not live long there. You will be completely destroyed.
27 Then the LORD will scatter you among the nations. Only a few of you will be left among the nations where the LORD will drive you.
28 There you will serve gods of wood and stone, made by people. These gods cannot see or hear or eat or smell.
29 But from there you will seek the LORD your God. You will find Him if you seek Him with all your heart and with all your soul.
30 When you are in trouble and all these things happen to you, then in later days you will return to the LORD your God. You will listen to His voice.
31 For the LORD your God is a merciful God. He will not leave you or destroy you. He will not forget the covenant with your fathers, which He promised them with an oath.
32 Ask now about the days long before you, from the day God created man on the earth. Ask from one end of the sky to the other. Has anything as great as this ever happened or been told?
33 Has any people ever heard the voice of God speaking out of the fire, as you have, and lived?

34 Has any god ever tried to take a nation out of another nation for himself? The LORD did this for you with tests, signs, wonders, and war. He used a strong hand and a stretched-out arm, and great fearful acts. He did it in Egypt, before your own eyes.
35 You were shown these things so that you would know one thing. The LORD is God; there is no other God.
36 He let you hear His voice from heaven to teach you. On earth He showed you His great fire. You heard His words out of the fire.
37 Because He loved your fathers, He chose their children after them. He brought you out of Egypt by being with you in great power.
38 He pushed out nations bigger and stronger than you before you. He brought you into their land. He gave it to you for your inheritance, as it is today.
39 Know today and take to heart that the LORD is God. He is God in heaven above and on the earth below. There is no other.

40 Keep His statutes and commandments, which I am giving you today. Then you and your children after you will live well. You will live long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you for all time.
41 Then Moses set apart three cities across the Jordan to the east.
42 A person who killed someone by accident, not on purpose, could run to one of these cities. There he could save his own life.
43 The cities were: Bezer in the wilderness on the high flat land, for the Reubenites; Ramoth in Gilead, for the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan, for the Manassites.
44 This is the law that Moses set before the Israelites.
45 These are the testimonies, statutes, and rules that Moses spoke to them after they had come out of Egypt.

46 They were in the valley across the Jordan, facing Beth-peor. This was in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon. Moses and the Israelites had defeated him after they came out of Egypt.
47 They took the land of Sihon and of Og king of Bashan — the two Amorite kings across the Jordan to the east.
48 Their land went from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley as far as Mount Siyon (that is, Hermon).
49 It included all the Arabah on the east side of the Jordan, as far as the Sea of the Arabah, below the slopes of Pisgah.

title: Deuteronomy 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses called all Israel together. He said to them: "Hear, O Israel, the rules and laws I tell you today. Learn them and obey them carefully.
2 The LORD our God made a covenant with us at Mount Horeb.
3 It was not with our ancestors that the LORD made this covenant. He made it with us — with all of us who are alive here today.
4 The LORD spoke face to face with you out of the fire on the mountain.
5 At that time I stood between the LORD and you. I was there to declare to you the word of the LORD. You were afraid of the fire and would not go up the mountain. And He said:
6 'I am the LORD your God. I brought you out of Egypt, out of the land of slavery.
7 You shall have no other gods before me.
8 You shall not make for yourself an image of anything in the sky above, on the earth below, or in the waters under the earth.

9 You shall not bow down to them or worship them. For I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God. I punish the children for the sin of the fathers, to the third and fourth generation of those who hate me.
10 But I show love to a thousand generations of those who love me and keep my commandments.
11 You shall not misuse the name of the LORD your God. The LORD will not leave anyone unpunished who misuses his name.
12 Keep the Sabbath day holy, as the LORD your God has commanded you.
13 Six days you shall labor and do all your work.

14 But the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God. On that day you must not do any work. This applies to you, your son or daughter, your male or female servant, your ox or donkey, all your farm animals, and the foreigner who lives in your town. Your male and female servants must rest as you do.

15 Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt. The LORD your God brought you out from there. He did it with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm. That is why the LORD your God has commanded you to keep the Sabbath day.
16 Honor your father and your mother, as the LORD your God has commanded you. Then you will live long. It will go well with you in the land the LORD your God gives you.
17 You shall not murder.
18 You shall not commit adultery.
19 You shall not steal.
20 You shall not give false testimony against your neighbor.
21 You shall not covet your neighbor's wife. You shall not covet your neighbor's house or field, his male or female servant, his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.'

22 The LORD spoke these commandments with a loud voice to your whole assembly. He spoke from the fire, the cloud, and the thick darkness on the mountain. He added nothing more. Then He wrote them on two stone tablets and gave them to me.
23 When you heard the voice out of the darkness, the mountain was burning with fire. All the leaders of your tribes and your elders came to me.
24 You said, "Look, the LORD our God has shown us His glory and greatness. We have heard His voice out of the fire. Today we have seen that a person can live even when God speaks with him.
25 But now, why should we die? This great fire will burn us up. We will die if we hear the voice of the LORD our God any longer.
26 Who of all humans has ever heard the voice of the living God out of the fire? Who has heard it as we have, and lived?
27 Go near and listen to all that the LORD our God says. Then tell us everything the LORD our God tells you. We will listen and obey."
28 The LORD heard what you said to me. He said to me, "I have heard the words these people have spoken to you. They have done well in all they have said.
29 I wish they had such a heart to fear me and keep all my commandments always! Then it would go well with them and their children forever.
30 Go and tell them, 'Go back to your tents.'
31 But you stay here with me. I will tell you all the commandments, rules, and laws you are to teach them. They will follow them in the land I am giving them to own."
32 So be careful to do as the LORD your God has commanded you. Do not turn away to the right or to the left.
33 Walk in obedience to all that the LORD your God has commanded you. Then you may live and do well. You will have long days in the land that you will own.

title: Deuteronomy 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 The LORD your God told me to teach you these commandments, decrees and laws. Follow them in the land you will enter and take.
2 Then you, your children and grandchildren will fear the LORD your God all your lives. Keep all his decrees and commandments, so your days may be long.
3 Hear, O Israel, and be careful to obey them. Then all will go well. You will grow many in a land flowing with milk and honey. This is what the LORD, the God of your fathers, promised you.
4 Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.
5 Love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength.
6 These commandments that I give you today are to be on your hearts.
7 Impress them on your children. Talk about them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up.
8 Tie them as symbols on your hands and bind them on your foreheads.
9 Write them on the doorframes of your houses and on your gates.
10 The LORD your God will bring you into the land. He promised it to your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. He will give you great and fine cities you did not build.
11 He will give you houses full of good things you did not fill. He will give you wells you did not dig. He will give you vineyards and olive trees you did not plant. You will eat and be full.
12 Then be careful. Do not forget the LORD. He brought you out of Egypt, out of the land of slavery.
13 Fear the LORD your God, serve him only and take your oaths in his name.
14 Do not follow other gods, the gods of the peoples around you.
15 For the LORD your God, who is with you, is a jealous God. His anger will burn against you and wipe you off the earth.
16 Do not put the LORD your God to the test as you did at Massah.
17 Be careful to keep the commandments of the LORD your God. Keep the rules and decrees he has given you.
18 Do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD. Then it will go well with you. You will enter and take the good land he promised your fathers.
19 He will drive out all your enemies before you, just as the LORD has said.
20 In the future, when your son asks you, "What is the meaning of the stipulations, decrees and laws the LORD our God has commanded you?"
21 tell him: "We were slaves of Pharaoh in Egypt, but the LORD brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand.
22 Before our eyes the LORD sent great and terrible signs and wonders on Egypt, on Pharaoh, and on all his house.
23 But he brought us out from there to lead us in and give us the land he had promised our fathers.
24 And the LORD commanded us to obey all these decrees and to fear the LORD our God, so that we might always prosper and be kept alive, as is the case today.
25 And if we are careful to obey all this law before the LORD our God, as he commanded us, that will be our righteousness."

title: Deuteronomy 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 When the LORD your God brings you into the land to take it, He will push out many nations before you. He will push out the Hittites, Girgashites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites — seven nations bigger and stronger than you.
2 When the LORD your God gives them to you, you must destroy them completely. Make no agreement with them. Show them no kindness.
3 Do not marry them. Do not give your daughters to their sons. Do not take their daughters for your sons.
4 They will turn your sons away from Me to serve other gods. Then the LORD will be angry with you and destroy you quickly.
5 Instead, do this to them: tear down their altars. Smash their sacred stones. Cut down their Asherah poles. Burn their idols in the fire.
6 You are a people holy to the LORD your God. The LORD your God has chosen you to be His special people, out of all peoples on the earth.
7 The LORD did not give you His love and choose you because you were more in number. You were the smallest of all peoples.
8 But the LORD loved you and kept the promise He made to your fathers. So He brought you out with a strong hand. He rescued you from the place of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.
9 So know that the LORD your God is God. He is the faithful God. He keeps His covenant of faithful love for a thousand generations of those who love Him and keep His commands.
10 But He pays back those who hate Him with destruction. He will not delay to pay back the one who hates Him.
11 So keep the commands and rules and laws that I am giving you today. Follow them.
12 If you listen to these laws and keep them carefully, the LORD your God will keep His covenant. He will keep the faithful love He swore to your fathers.

13 He will love you and bless you and make you many. He will bless the children you have and the crops of your land. He will bless your grain, new wine, and oil. He will bless your young cows and lambs in the land He promised your fathers.
14 You will be blessed more than all peoples. No man or woman among you will be unable to have children. Your farm animals will be the same.
15 The LORD will take away from you all sickness. He will not put on you the terrible diseases you knew in Egypt. But He will send them on all who hate you.
16 You must destroy all the peoples the LORD your God gives you. Do not look at them with pity. Do not worship their gods. That will be a trap for you.
17 You may say in your heart, "These nations are stronger than we are. How can we push them out?"
18 But do not be afraid of them. Remember what the LORD your God did to Pharaoh and all Egypt.

19 Remember the great tests you saw. Remember the signs and wonders, the strong hand and strong arm by which the LORD your God brought you out. The LORD your God will do the same to all the peoples you fear now.
20 Also, the LORD your God will send the hornet against them. Even those who hide from you will die.
21 Do not be afraid of them. The LORD your God is among you. He is a great and powerful God.
22 The LORD your God will push out these nations before you little by little. You cannot remove them all at once. If you do, the wild animals will grow too many around you.
23 But the LORD your God will give them to you. He will throw them into great confusion until they are destroyed.
24 He will give their kings to you. You will wipe out their names from under heaven. No one will be able to stand against you. You will destroy them completely.
25 You must burn the images of their gods. Do not want for yourself the silver and gold on them. Do not take it, or you will be trapped by it. It is hated by the LORD your God.
26 Do not bring any hated thing into your house. If you do, you will be marked for destruction like it. Hate it strongly. It is marked for destruction.

title: Deuteronomy 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 You must carefully obey every command I give you today. Then you will live and grow in number. You will enter and take the land that the LORD promised to your fathers.
2 Remember how the LORD your God led you in the desert for forty years. He did this to make you humble and to test you. He wanted to know your heart, whether you would obey His commands.

3 He made you humble. He let you go hungry. Then He fed you with manna, which you and your fathers had never known. He did this to teach you that **Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of the LORD**.
4 Your clothes did not wear out. Your feet did not swell during these forty years.
5 So know in your heart this truth. Just as a father trains his son, the LORD your God trains you.
6 So keep the commands of the LORD your God. Walk in His ways and fear Him.
7 The LORD your God is bringing you into a good land. It is a land of streams, pools, and springs. They flow through the valleys and hills.
8 It is a land of wheat, barley, vines, fig trees, and pomegranates. It is a land of olive oil and honey.
9 In this land you will eat food and never lack any. You will have all you need. Its rocks have iron. You can dig copper from its hills.
10 When you eat and are full, bless the LORD your God. Thank Him for the good land He has given you.
11 Be careful not to forget the LORD your God. Do not fail to keep His commands, rules, and laws that I give you today.
12 When you eat and are full, you may build fine houses to live in.
13 Your cattle and sheep may grow many. Your silver and gold may grow. All that you have may grow.
14 Then your heart may become proud. You may forget the LORD your God. He brought you out of Egypt, out of the land where you were slaves.
15 He led you through the huge and scary desert. It had poisonous snakes and scorpions. It was a dry land with no water. He brought you water from hard rock.
16 He fed you in the desert with manna, which your fathers had not known. He did this to make you humble and test you. In the end He wanted you to do well.
17 You may say in your heart, "My own power and strong hands have made this wealth for me."
18 But remember the LORD your God. He gives you the power to gain wealth. He does this to keep His covenant. He promised it to your fathers, and He keeps it today.
19 If you ever forget the LORD your God, you will follow other gods. You will worship them and bow down to them. I warn you today that you will surely be destroyed.
20 The LORD has destroyed nations before you. In the same way, you will be destroyed if you do not obey the LORD your God.

title: Deuteronomy 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Hear, O Israel! Today you will cross the Jordan. You will take the land from nations bigger and stronger than you, with great cities and walls that reach the sky.
2 The people are strong and tall. They are the children of the Anakim. You know about them. You have heard people say, "Who can stand against the sons of Anak?"

3 But understand this today: the LORD your God goes ahead of you. He is like a fire that burns up everything. He will destroy them and bring them down before you. You will drive them out and wipe them out quickly, as the LORD has promised.
4 When the LORD your God drives them out, do not say in your heart, "The LORD brought me here because I am right with God." No — the LORD is driving these nations out because of their evil.

5 You are not going in because you are right with God or have a good heart. The LORD your God is driving these nations out because they are evil. He is also keeping the promise He made to your fathers — to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
6 So understand: the LORD your God is not giving you this good land because you are right with God. You are a stubborn people.
7 Remember this. Never forget how you made the LORD your God angry in the wilderness. From the day you left Egypt until you came here, you have been turning against the LORD.
8 At Horeb you made the LORD angry. He was angry enough to destroy you.

9 I went up on the mountain to receive the stone tablets — the tablets of the covenant that the LORD made with you. I stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights. I ate no bread and drank no water.
10 Then the LORD gave me the two stone tablets. God's own finger wrote on them. They had the exact words that the LORD spoke to you from the fire on the day of the meeting.
11 At the end of forty days and forty nights, the LORD gave me the two stone tablets — the tablets of the covenant.

12 Then the LORD said to me, "Get up and go down from here at once. Your people, whom you brought out of Egypt, have ruined themselves. They have turned away so quickly from the way I commanded them. They have made a metal idol for themselves."
13 The LORD also said to me, "I have watched this people. They are truly a stubborn people.
14 Leave Me alone, so I can destroy them and wipe out their name from under the sky. Then I will make you into a nation bigger and stronger than they are."
15 So I went back down the mountain. The mountain was burning with fire. I had the two tablets of the covenant in my hands.
16 I saw how you had sinned against the LORD your God. You had made a metal calf for yourselves. You had turned away quickly from the way the LORD had commanded you.
17 So I took the two tablets and threw them from my hands. I broke them to pieces in front of your eyes.

18 Then I fell down before the LORD for forty days and forty nights, as I had done before. I did not eat bread or drink water. This was because of all the sin you had done. You did evil in the eyes of the LORD and made Him angry.
19 I was afraid of the LORD's great anger against you. He was angry enough to destroy you. But the LORD listened to me this time too.
20 The LORD was also angry enough with Aaron to destroy him. But at that time I also prayed for Aaron.

21 I took the sinful thing — the calf you had made — and burned it in the fire. Then I crushed it and ground it to powder as fine as dust. I threw the dust into the stream that came down from the mountain.
22 You also made the LORD angry at Taberah, at Massah, and at Kibroth-hattaavah.
23 When the LORD sent you out from Kadesh-barnea, He said, "Go up and take the land I have given you." But you turned against the command of the LORD your God. You did not trust Him or obey Him.
24 You have been turning against the LORD since the day I first knew you.
25 So I fell down before the LORD for forty days and forty nights. I did this because the LORD had said He would destroy you.
26 I prayed to the LORD and said, "O Lord GOD, do not destroy Your people. They are Your special people. You saved them by Your great power. You brought them out of Egypt with a strong hand.
27 Remember Your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Do not look at how stubborn this people is, or at their evil sin.

28 If You destroy them, the people in the land You brought us from will say, 'The LORD could not bring them into the land He promised. He hated them, so He brought them out to kill them in the wilderness.'
29 But they are Your people. They are Your special people. You brought them out by Your great power and Your powerful arm."

title: Deuteronomy 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 At that time the LORD said to me, "Cut two stone tablets like the first ones. Come up to me on the mountain. Make a wooden box too."
2 "I will write on the tablets the same words from the first tablets, which you broke. Place them in the box."
3 So I made a box of acacia wood. I cut two stone tablets like the first ones. I went up the mountain with the two tablets in my hands.
4 The LORD wrote on the tablets the same words as before. These were the Ten Commandments. He spoke them to you from the fire on the mountain. The LORD gave them to me.
5 I went back down the mountain. I placed the tablets in the box I had made, as the LORD commanded me. They have stayed there.
6 The Israelites traveled from Beeroth Bene-jaakan to Moserah. Aaron died there and was buried. His son Eleazar became priest after him.
7 From there they traveled to Gudgodah. From Gudgodah they went to Jotbathah, a land with streams of water.

8 At that time the LORD chose the tribe of Levi. They would carry the ark of the covenant of the LORD. They would stand before the LORD to serve him. They would bless the people in his name, as they still do today.
9 That is why Levi has no share or land among his brothers. The LORD is his special gift, as the LORD your God promised him.
10 I stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights, like the first time. The LORD listened to me again. He agreed not to destroy you.
11 Then the LORD said to me, "Get up. Continue your journey ahead of the people. They will enter and take the land. I promised this land to their fathers."

12 And now, O Israel, what does the LORD your God ask of you? He asks you to fear the LORD your God. Walk in all his ways. Love him. Serve the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.
13 Keep the commandments and rules of the LORD that I give you today. They are for your own good.
14 Look — the heavens belong to the LORD your God, even the highest heavens. The earth and everything in it also belong to him.
15 Yet the LORD set his love on your fathers and loved them. He chose you, their children after them, above all the peoples, even today.
16 **Circumcise your hearts, therefore, and do not be stiff-necked any longer.**
17 **For the LORD your God is God of gods and Lord of lords, the great God, mighty and awesome, who shows no partiality and accepts no bribes.**
18 **He defends the cause of the fatherless and the widow, and loves the foreigner residing among you, giving them food and clothing.**
19 So you also must love the foreigner. You yourselves were foreigners in the land of Egypt.
20 **Fear the LORD your God, serve him and hold fast to him.** Make your promises in his name.
21 He is your praise. He is your God. He has done great wonders for you. Your own eyes have seen them.
22 Your fathers went down to Egypt, seventy people in all. And now the LORD your God has made you as many as the stars in the sky.

title: Deuteronomy 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 So you must love the LORD your God. Always keep his rules, laws, and commandments.
2 Remember today: your children have not seen the LORD's training. You have seen his greatness, his strong hand, and his powerful arm.
3 You saw the signs and great acts he did in Egypt. He did them to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and to all his land.
4 You saw what he did to the Egyptian army, horses, and war chariots. He made the Red Sea cover them as they chased you. He destroyed them completely, even to this day.
5 You saw what he did for you in the wilderness until you came to this place.
6 You saw what he did to Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab the Reubenite. He did this in front of all Israel. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them.
7 It swallowed their families, their tents, and every living thing they owned. Your own eyes have seen every great work the LORD has done.
8 So you must keep every commandment I am giving you today. Then you will be strong enough to cross the Jordan and take the land.
9 Then you will live long in the land. The LORD promised this land to your fathers and their descendants. It is a rich land, full of milk and honey.
10 The land you are entering is not like the land of Egypt. There you planted seeds and watered them by foot, like a vegetable garden.
11 But the land you are crossing the Jordan to take is different. It is a land of mountains and valleys. It gets rain from the sky.
12 The LORD your God cares for this land. His eyes watch over it from the start to the end of the year.
13 So obey the commandments I am giving you today. Love the LORD your God. Serve him with all your heart and soul.
14 Then I will send rain for your land in the right season. I will send rains in autumn and spring. You will gather grain, new wine, and olive oil.
15 I will give grass in the fields for your farm animals. You will eat and be full.
16 But be careful. Do not let anyone tempt you to turn away. Do not worship or bow down to other gods.
17 If you do, the LORD will become angry with you. He will shut the sky so no rain will fall. The land will give no crops. You will soon die in the good land the LORD is giving you.
18 Fix these words of mine in your hearts and minds. Tie them on your hands as reminders. Tie them on your foreheads.
19 Teach them to your children. Speak about them when you sit at home and walk along the road. Speak about them when you lie down and when you get up.
20 Write them on the door frames of your houses and on your gates.
21 Then your days will be many in the land. Your children's days will also be many. This will last as long as the sky is above the earth. The LORD promised this land to your fathers.
22 Carefully keep all these commandments I am giving you. Love the LORD your God. Walk in all his ways. Hold on to him closely.
23 Then the LORD will drive out all these nations before you. You will take the land from nations bigger and stronger than you.
24 Every place where your foot steps will be yours. Your land will reach from the wilderness to Lebanon. It will reach from the Euphrates River to the Western Sea.
25 No one will be able to stand against you. The LORD your God will put fear and terror of you on all the land. This will happen wherever you set foot, as he promised.
26 Look, today I am setting before you a blessing and a curse —
27 a blessing if you obey the commandments of the LORD your God that I am giving you today,
28 but a curse if you disobey them. A curse comes if you turn away from the path I command you today. A curse comes if you follow other gods you have not known.
29 The LORD your God will bring you into the land to take it. When he does, announce the blessing on Mount Gerizim. Announce the curse on Mount Ebal.
30 These mountains are across the Jordan, west of the road toward the sunset. They are in the land of the Canaanites who live in the Arabah, near Gilgal, by the Oak of Moreh.
31 You are about to cross the Jordan. You will enter and take the land the LORD your God is giving you. You will take it and settle in it.
32 Be careful to follow all the rules and laws I am setting before you today.

title: Deuteronomy 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 These are the laws and rules you must obey. Follow them all your days in the land the LORD has given you.
2 Destroy every place where the other nations served their gods. Destroy the high mountains, the hills, and every green tree.
3 Tear down their altars. Smash their holy stones. Burn their Asherah poles. Cut down their idols. Wipe out their names from every place.
4 You must not worship the LORD your God in their way.
5 Instead, the LORD will choose one place from your tribes for His Name. You must go there.
6 Bring your burnt offerings and sacrifices to that place. Bring your tithes, special gifts, vow offerings, and freewill offerings. Bring the first young of your cattle and sheep.
7 There you and your family will eat before the LORD your God. You will be glad in all you do, because He has blessed you.
8 You must not act as we do here today. Right now, everyone does what seems right to himself.
9 You have not yet come to the place of rest. You have not yet received the gift of land.
10 Soon you will cross the Jordan. You will live in the land the LORD gives you. He will give you rest from all your enemies, and you will live safely.
11 Then the LORD your God will choose a place for His Name. Bring everything I command you there. Bring your burnt offerings, sacrifices, tithes, and special gifts. Bring all your best vow offerings.
12 Be glad before the LORD your God. Bring your sons and daughters, your male and female servants. Bring the Levite in your town, because he has no land of his own.
13 Be careful not to offer your burnt offerings in just any place you see.
14 You must offer them only at the place the LORD will choose. He will choose it in one of your tribal lands. Do all I command you there.
15 But you may kill and eat meat in your town whenever you want. The LORD your God has blessed you with this food. Both clean and unclean people may eat it, like gazelle or deer.
16 But you must not eat the blood. Pour it on the ground like water.

17 In your town you must not eat your tithe of grain, new wine, or oil. You must not eat the first young of your cattle or sheep. You must not eat your vow offerings, freewill offerings, or special gifts.

18 Instead, eat them before the LORD your God at the place He will choose. Eat with your sons, daughters, and male and female servants. Eat with the Levite in your town. Be glad before the LORD your God in all you do.
19 Be careful not to forget the Levites as long as you live in your land.
20 The LORD your God will make your land bigger, as He promised. When you want meat, you may say, "I want to eat meat." You may eat it whenever you want.
21 The place the LORD chose for His Name may be far from you. Then you may kill any of your cattle or sheep, as I commanded. You may eat it in your town whenever you want.
22 You may eat it as you would eat a gazelle or deer. Both clean and unclean people may eat it.
23 Only be sure not to eat the blood. The blood is the life. You must not eat the life with the meat.
24 You must not eat the blood. Pour it on the ground like water.
25 Do not eat it, so things will go well with you and your children. You will be doing what is right before the LORD.
26 But take your holy things and your vow offerings. Go to the place the LORD will choose.
27 Place the meat and blood of your burnt offerings on the altar of the LORD your God. Pour the blood of your other sacrifices beside the altar. You may eat the meat.
28 Be careful to obey all these things I command you. Then things will always go well with you and your children. You will be doing what is good and right before the LORD your God.
29 The LORD your God will cut off the nations in front of you. You will drive them out and live in their land.
30 Then be careful not to be trapped by their ways. Do not ask, "How do these nations serve their gods? I will do the same."
31 You must not worship the LORD your God in their way. They do every hated thing the LORD hates for their gods. They even burn their sons and daughters in fire as sacrifices.
32 See that you do all I command you. Do not add to it or take away from it.

title: Deuteronomy 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 A prophet or dreamer may come from among you. He may tell you a sign or wonder.
2 The sign or wonder may come true. But he may say, "Let us follow other gods you have not known. Let us worship them."
3 Do not listen to that prophet or dreamer. The LORD your God is testing you. He wants to see if you love Him with all your heart and soul.
4 Follow the LORD your God and fear Him. Keep His commands and listen to His voice. Serve Him and stay close to Him.

5 That prophet or dreamer must be put to death. He led people to turn against the LORD your God. The LORD brought you out of Egypt. He set you free from the house of slavery. That prophet tried to turn you from the LORD's way. So remove the evil from among you.

6 Your own brother, son, or daughter may secretly try to lead you astray. Your dear wife or closest friend may do this too. They may say, "Let us go and worship other gods." These gods are unknown to you and your fathers.
7 These are the gods of the peoples around you. They may be near or far, from one end of the earth to the other.
8 Do not give in to him. Do not listen to him. Show him no pity. Do not spare him or hide him.
9 Instead, you must surely kill him. Your hand must be the first to strike him. Then all the people must join.
10 Throw stones at him to kill him. He tried to turn you from the LORD your God. The LORD brought you out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
11 Then all Israel will hear and be afraid. They will never do such an evil thing again.
12 You may hear news about one of your cities. The LORD your God gave you these cities to live in.
13 Wicked men may come from among you. They may lead their city away from God. They may say, "Let us go and serve other gods you have not known."
14 Then you must ask, check, and search carefully. You must find out if this hated thing has truly been done among you.
15 Then you must kill the people of that city with the sword. Destroy it completely, with its people and farm animals.

16 Gather all its goods in the middle of the public square. Burn the city and all its goods completely. Offer it as a whole burnt offering to the LORD your God. The city must stay a pile of ruins forever. It must never be rebuilt.

17 Keep nothing that the LORD has set apart for destruction. Then the LORD will turn from His strong anger. He will give you mercy and show you kindness. He will make you a great people, as He promised your fathers.
18 This will happen because you obey the LORD your God. Keep all His commands I give you today. Do what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

title: Deuteronomy 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 You are children of the LORD your God. Do not cut your bodies or shave the front of your heads for the dead.
2 You are a holy people for the LORD your God. The LORD has chosen you as His special treasure from all the peoples on the earth.
3 You must not eat any hateful food.
4 These are the animals that you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
5 the deer, the gazelle, the roe deer, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope, and the mountain sheep.
6 You may eat any animal with a hoof split into two parts that also brings food back up to chew again.
7 But you must not eat these, even if they chew again or have a split hoof: the camel, the rabbit, or the rock badger.
8 They chew again but do not have a split hoof. They are not clean for you. The pig also has a split hoof but does not chew again. It is not clean for you. Do not eat its meat or touch its dead body.
9 Of all the things that live in the water, you may eat anything with fins and scales.
10 But you may not eat anything without fins and scales. It is not clean for you.
11 You may eat any clean bird.
12 But you must not eat these: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
13 the red kite, the falcon, any kind of kite,
14 any kind of raven,
15 the ostrich, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
16 the little owl, the great owl, the white owl,
17 the desert owl, the osprey, the cormorant,
18 the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe, or the bat.
19 All flying insects are not clean for you. You may not eat them.
20 But you may eat any clean bird.

21 Do not eat any animal that has died on its own. You may give it to the foreigner who lives in your town, and he may eat it. Or you may sell it to a foreigner. You are a holy people for the LORD your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
22 Each year you must give a tenth of all the food from your fields.

23 Eat a tenth of your grain, new wine, and oil before the LORD your God. Also bring the first young animals from your cattle and sheep. Eat them at the place He will choose as a home for His Name. This will teach you to respect the LORD your God always.
24 The place may be too far from you. You may not be able to carry the food the LORD your God has given you.
25 Then change it for money. Take the money in your hand. Go to the place the LORD your God will choose.
26 There you may spend the money on anything you want. Buy cattle, sheep, wine, strong wine, or anything you wish. Eat there before the LORD your God and be glad with your family.
27 Do not forget the Levite who lives in your town. He has no land or share among you.
28 At the end of every three years, bring a tenth of all your food for that year. Store it in your town.

29 Then the Levite (who has no land or share among you), the foreigner, children with no father, and widows in your town may come and eat and be filled. And the LORD your God will bless you in all the work of your hands.

title: Deuteronomy 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 At the end of every seven years, you must cancel debts.
2 This is how it works. Every person who has loaned money must cancel what his neighbor owes. He must not ask his neighbor or brother to pay. The LORD has called this a time of release.
3 You may collect from a foreigner. But you must forgive what your brother owes you.
4 There will be no poor people among you. The LORD will bless you in the land he is giving you as your own.
5 But you must obey the LORD your God. You must follow all the commands I give you today.
6 The LORD your God will bless you, just as he promised. You will lend to many nations but borrow from none. You will rule over many nations, but none will rule over you.
7 A poor brother may live in one of your towns in the land. Then do not close your heart or your hand to him.
8 Instead, open your hand to him. Lend him freely whatever he needs.

9 Do not hold this evil thought in your heart: "The seventh year, the year of release, is near." Then you might look at your poor brother with a mean look and give him nothing. He will cry out to the LORD against you, and you will be guilty of sin.
10 Give to him with a willing heart. Do not feel sad when you give. Because of this, the LORD your God will bless all your work.
11 There will always be poor people in the land. So I command you to open your hand wide. Help your brother and the poor and needy in your land.
12 A fellow Hebrew, a man or a woman, may be sold to you. After he serves you six years, you must set him free in the seventh year.
13 When you set him free, do not send him away with empty hands.
14 Give him many gifts from your flock, your grain floor, and your wine press. Give to him as the LORD your God has blessed you.
15 Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt. The LORD your God set you free. That is why I give you this command today.
16 But your servant may say to you, "I do not want to leave you." He says this because he loves you and your family and is happy with you.
17 Then take a sharp tool and push it through his ear into the door. He will be your servant for life. Treat your female servant the same way.
18 Do not feel it is hard to set your servant free. His six years of work were worth twice the wages of a hired worker. And the LORD your God will bless you in all you do.
19 You must give to the LORD your God every firstborn male from your herds and flocks. Do not put the firstborn of your oxen to work. Do not cut the wool of the firstborn of your flock.
20 Each year, you and your family must eat it before the LORD your God. Eat it in the place the LORD will choose.
21 But the animal may be hurt, cannot walk, cannot see, or has a bad problem. Then you must not offer it to the LORD your God.
22 Eat it inside your towns. Both clean and unclean people may eat it, like a wild deer.
23 But you must not eat the blood. Pour it on the ground like water.

title: Deuteronomy 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Keep the month of Abib. Celebrate the Passover to the LORD your God. In the month of Abib, the LORD your God brought you out of Egypt at night.
2 Offer the Passover sacrifice to the LORD your God from your herd or flock. Do this in the place the LORD will choose as a home for his Name.
3 Do not eat bread with yeast with the sacrifice. For seven days, eat bread without yeast — the bread of suffering. You left Egypt in a hurry. So you will remember that day all your life.
4 No yeast may be found in all your land for seven days. Do not keep any meat from the evening sacrifice until morning.
5 Do not offer the Passover animal in any town the LORD your God gives you.
6 Offer the Passover sacrifice only at the place the LORD your God will choose as a home for his Name. Do this in the evening, when the sun sets. This is the time you left Egypt.
7 Roast it and eat it in the place the LORD your God will choose. In the morning, return to your tents.
8 For six days, eat bread without yeast. On the seventh day, hold a special meeting to the LORD your God. Do not do any work.
9 Count off seven weeks. Start counting when you first cut the standing grain.
10 Then celebrate the Feast of Weeks to the LORD your God. Bring a free gift. Give as the LORD your God has blessed you.

11 Be happy before the LORD your God in the place he will choose as a home for his Name. Rejoice with your sons and daughters, your male and female servants, and the Levite in your town. Rejoice with the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow among you.
12 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt. Carefully follow these rules.
13 Celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles for seven days. Do this after you gather grain from your grain floor and your wine press.
14 Be happy at your feast — you, your sons and daughters, your male and female servants, and the Levite. Rejoice with the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow among you.
15 For seven days, celebrate a feast to the LORD your God in the place he will choose. The LORD your God will bless all your crops and all your work. So your joy will be full.

16 Three times a year, all your men must appear before the LORD your God in the place he will choose. They will come at the Feast of Bread Without Yeast, the Feast of Weeks, and the Feast of Tabernacles. No one should appear before the LORD with empty hands.
17 Everyone must bring a gift. Give as you are able, by the blessing the LORD your God has given you.
18 Appoint judges and officers for your tribes in every town the LORD your God gives you. They must judge the people with fair judgment.
19 Do not deny justice. Do not favor one side. Do not take a bribe. A bribe blinds the eyes of the wise. It twists the words of the righteous.
20 Follow justice, and justice alone. Then you will live. You will keep the land the LORD your God is giving you.
21 Do not set up any wooden Asherah pole next to the altar you build for the LORD your God.
22 Do not set up a holy stone pillar for yourself. The LORD your God hates this.

title: Deuteronomy 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Do not offer to the LORD your God an ox or sheep with anything wrong with it. That is hateful to the LORD your God.
2 A man or woman among you may do evil before the LORD your God. They may break his covenant.
3 They may go and worship other gods. They may bow down to them, or to the sun, moon, or stars. I have not allowed this.
4 If you hear a report about it, you must check it carefully. If the report is true and this terrible thing has happened in Israel,
5 you must bring that man or woman out to your gates. You must stone that person to death.
6 A person can be put to death only if two or three witnesses speak against him. One witness alone is not enough.
7 The witnesses must throw the first stones. Then all the people must join in. You must remove the evil from among you.
8 A case may be too hard for you to judge. It may be about killing, lawsuits, or fights at your gates. Then you must go up to the place the LORD your God will choose.
9 Go to the Levitical priests and to the judge who serves at that time. Ask them, and they will give you a decision.
10 You must follow the decision they give you at the place the LORD will choose. Do everything they teach you.
11 Follow the law they give and the decision they speak. Do not turn aside, to the right or to the left.
12 A man may act with pride and refuse to listen to the priest or the judge. That man must be put to death. You must remove the evil from Israel.
13 Then all the people will hear and be afraid. They will no longer act with pride.
14 You will enter the land that the LORD your God is giving you. You will take it and live in it. Then you may say, "Let us choose a king to rule us, like all the nations around us."
15 You must choose the king whom the LORD your God picks. Choose a king from among your brothers. Do not put a foreigner over you.
16 The king must not get many horses for himself. He must not send the people back to Egypt to get more horses. The LORD has said, "You must never go back that way again."
17 He must not take many wives, or his heart will turn away from God. He must not collect large amounts of silver and gold.
18 When he sits on his king's throne, he must write a copy of this law on a scroll. He must do this in front of the Levitical priests.
19 He must keep it with him. He must read from it every day of his life. Then he will learn to fear the LORD his God. He will carefully obey all the words of this law and these rules.
20 Then he will not feel better than his people. He will not turn aside from the command, to the right or to the left. He and his sons will rule many years over his kingdom in Israel.

title: Deuteronomy 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 The Levitical priests—the whole tribe of Levi—will have no share or land in Israel. They will eat the offerings burned for the LORD. That is their inheritance.
2 They have no land among their brothers. The LORD is their inheritance, as He promised them.
3 This is the priests' share from the people who offer a sacrifice. From a bull or sheep, give the priests the shoulder, the cheeks, and the stomach.
4 Give them the firstfruits of your grain, new wine, and oil. Also give the first wool from your sheep.
5 The LORD your God chose Levi and his sons from all your tribes. They will stand and serve in His name forever.
6 A Levite may move from any town in Israel. He may come with all his heart to the place the LORD will choose.
7 Then he will serve in the name of the LORD his God. He will serve like his fellow Levites who stand there before the LORD.
8 They will eat equal shares. This is true even if he has money from selling his father's land.
9 You will enter the land the LORD your God is giving you. Do not copy the terrible ways of the nations there.
10 No one among you must burn his son or daughter in the fire. No one must use magic or fortune-telling or read signs or do witchcraft.
11 No one must put spells on people. No one must ask a medium or spirit-guide. No one must ask the dead.
12 Whoever does these things is hateful to the LORD. Because of these terrible things, the LORD your God is driving out the nations before you.
13 You must be without fault before the LORD your God.
14 These nations, which you will drive out, listen to magicians and fortune-tellers. But the LORD your God has not allowed you to do this.
15 **The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your fellow Israelites. You must listen to him.**

16 This is what you asked of the LORD your God at Horeb. On the day of the gathering you said, "Let us not hear the voice of the LORD our God again. Let us not see this great fire anymore, or we will die!"
17 Then the LORD said to me, "They have spoken well.
18 I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. I will put My words in his mouth. He will tell them everything I command him.
19 I will call to account anyone who does not listen to My words. These are the words the prophet speaks in My name.
20 But a prophet may dare to speak a message in My name that I did not command. Or he may speak in the name of other gods. That prophet must be put to death."
21 You may ask in your heart, "How can we know a message that the LORD has not spoken?"
22 A prophet may speak in the name of the LORD. If the message does not happen or come true, the LORD did not speak it. The prophet spoke on his own. Do not be afraid of him.

title: Deuteronomy 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 The LORD your God will destroy the nations in this land. He is giving their land to you. You will drive them out. You will live in their cities and houses.
2 Then you must set apart three cities in the land. The LORD your God is giving this land to you.
3 You must build roads to them. Divide the land into three parts. Then anyone who kills a person can run to these cities.
4 Here is the rule for someone who kills a person and runs to one of these cities. He killed his neighbor by accident, not on purpose.

5 For example, a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood. He swings his axe to chop down a tree. But the axe head comes off the handle. It hits his neighbor and kills him. That man may run to one of these cities to save his life.
6 If not, the avenger of blood may chase him in great anger. He may catch him if the road is long. He may kill him, even though he did not deserve to die. The man did not mean to harm his neighbor.
7 This is why I am telling you to set apart three cities.
8 The LORD your God may make your land bigger, as He promised your fathers. He may give you all the land He promised them.
9 You must carefully obey all these commands I give you today. You must love the LORD your God. You must walk in His ways at all times. Then you must add three more cities to these three.
10 In this way, the blood of innocent people will not be spilled in your land. The LORD your God is giving this land to you as an inheritance. You will not be guilty of killing.
11 But suppose a man hates his neighbor. He waits in secret, attacks him, and kills him. Then he runs to one of these cities.
12 The elders of his city must send for him. They must bring him back. They must hand him over to the avenger of blood to die.
13 Do not feel sorry for him. Remove from Israel the guilt of killing innocent people. Then it will go well with you.
14 Do not move your neighbor's boundary stone. Your ancestors set it up. It marks the land you will receive. The LORD your God is giving you this land.
15 One witness is not enough to prove a sin or crime against a man. A case must be settled by what two or three witnesses say.
16 Suppose a false witness speaks against someone. He says that man did a crime.
17 Then both men in the dispute must stand before the LORD. They must stand before the priests and judges on duty at that time.
18 The judges must check the case very carefully. They may find that the witness lied. He falsely accused his brother.
19 Then you must do to him what he wanted to do to his brother. In this way, remove the evil from among you.
20 The rest of the people will hear about it and be afraid. They will never again do such an evil thing among you.
21 Do not feel sorry for him: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, and foot for foot.

title: Deuteronomy 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 When you go to war against your enemies, you may see horses, war wagons, and a bigger army. Do not be afraid of them. The LORD your God is with you. He brought you out of Egypt.
2 When you are about to fight, the priest must come forward and speak to the army.
3 He will say, "Listen, Israel! Today you go to fight your enemies. Do not be weak in heart or afraid. Do not be shaken or filled with fear.
4 The LORD your God goes with you. He will fight for you against your enemies. He will help you win."
5 Then the leaders will also speak to the army. They will say, "Has any man built a new house but not yet used it? Let him go home. He might die in battle, and another man would use it.
6 Has any man planted a vineyard but not yet eaten its fruit? Let him go home. He might die in battle, and another man would eat its fruit.
7 Has any man promised to marry a woman but not yet married her? Let him go home. He might die in battle, and another man would marry her."
8 Then the leaders will say more to the army. "Is any man afraid or weak in heart? Let him go home. His brothers must not lose courage like him."
9 When the leaders finish speaking, they will choose army leaders to lead the men.
10 When you come near a city to fight it, first offer peace to it.
11 If they accept your peace and open their gates, all the people will work for you.
12 But if they refuse peace and fight against you, surround that city.
13 When the LORD your God gives the city to you, kill every man with the sword.
14 But you may take the women, children, animals, and all the goods. The LORD your God gives you these things from your enemies.
15 Treat all far away cities this way. These cities do not belong to the nations near you.
16 But in the cities the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance, do not leave anything alive.
17 You must destroy them all completely — the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites. The LORD your God has commanded this.
18 If you let them live, they will teach you their terrible ways. They serve other gods. You would sin against the LORD your God.
19 When you surround a city for a long time, do not cut down its fruit trees. You can eat their fruit. Are the trees of the field people, that you should fight them?
20 But you may cut down trees that do not give fruit. Use them to build tools to attack the city until it falls.

title: Deuteronomy 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 A dead body may be found lying in a field. This is in the land the LORD your God gives you. No one knows who killed him.
2 Then your elders and judges must come out. They must measure the distance to the nearby towns.
3 The elders of the town nearest the body will take a young cow. It must have never been used for any work.
4 They will bring the young cow down to a valley with running water. The valley must not be farmed. There they will break its neck by the stream.
5 Then the priests, the sons of Levi, will come forward. The LORD your God chose them to serve him. They speak blessings in his name. They also judge every fight and every case of harm.
6 Then all the elders of the nearest town will wash their hands. They will do this by the stream, over the young cow with the broken neck.
7 They will say, "Our hands did not shed this blood. Our eyes did not see it.
8 O LORD, accept this atonement. Forgive your people Israel, whom you have set free. Do not hold this innocent blood against them." Then the blood guilt will be covered.
9 So you will remove from yourselves the guilt of innocent blood. You will do what is right in the eyes of the LORD.
10 When you go to war against your enemies, the LORD your God will give them to you. You will take them as prisoners.
11 You may see a beautiful woman among them. You may want her and wish to marry her.
12 Then you must bring her into your house. She must shave her head and cut her nails.

13 She must put away the clothes she wore as a captive. She will live in your house for a full month. She will cry for her father and mother. After that, you may become one with her. You will be her husband, and she will be your wife.
14 If she does not please you, you must let her go where she wishes. You must not sell her for money. You must not treat her as a slave. You have shamed her.
15 A man may have two wives. He loves one but does not love the other. Both have sons. The first son is from the wife he does not love.
16 That man will give his wealth to his sons one day. He must not make the loved wife's son the firstborn. The real firstborn is the son of the wife he does not love.
17 Instead, he must honor the true firstborn. He must give him a double share of all he has. That son is the first sign of his father's strength. The right of the firstborn belongs to him.
18 A man may have a stubborn and disobedient son. The son does not obey his father and mother. He does not listen even when they correct him.
19 Then his father and mother must take hold of him. They must bring him to the elders at the town gate.
20 They will say to the elders, "This son of ours is stubborn and disobedient. He does not obey us. He eats too much and drinks too much."
21 Then all the men of his town will stone him to death. So you must remove the evil from among you. All Israel will hear and be afraid.
22 A man may do a sin that deserves death, and he is killed. Then you may hang his body on a pole.

23 You must not leave the body on the pole all night. You must bury him that same day, because **anyone who is hung on a pole is under God's curse**. You must not make the land unclean. The LORD your God gives you that land as your inheritance.

title: Deuteronomy 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 If you see your brother's ox or sheep wandering away, do not pretend you did not see it. Take it back to your brother.
2 If your brother does not live near you, or you do not know him, take the animal home. Keep it until your brother comes to look for it. Then give it back to him.
3 Do the same for his donkey, his coat, or anything your brother has lost. If you find it, do not pretend you did not see it.
4 If you see your brother's donkey or ox fallen on the road, do not ignore it. Help him lift it up.
5 A woman must not wear men's clothes. A man must not wear women's clothes. Anyone who does this is hateful to the LORD your God.
6 Suppose you find a bird's nest along the road, in a tree or on the ground. There are baby birds or eggs in it, and the mother is sitting on them. Do not take the mother with the young.
7 You may take the young, but let the mother go. Then it will be well with you, and you will live a long life.
8 When you build a new house, put a low wall around your roof. Then no one will fall from it, and you will not bring guilt for that death on your house.
9 Do not plant your vineyard with two kinds of seed. If you do, the whole harvest will be unclean — both the seed you plant and the fruit of the vineyard.
10 Do not plow with an ox and a donkey tied together.
11 Do not wear clothes made of wool and linen woven together.
12 Make small tassels on the four corners of the coat you wear.
13 Suppose a man marries a woman and sleeps with her, but then he begins to hate her.
14 He accuses her of shameful behavior and gives her a bad name. He says, "I married this woman and slept with her, but she was not a virgin."
15 Then the young woman's father and mother shall bring the proof that she was a virgin to the leaders of the city at the gate.
16 The father shall say to the leaders, "I gave my daughter in marriage to this man, but he has come to hate her.

17 Now he has accused her of shameful behavior. He says, 'I found that your daughter was not a virgin.' But here is the proof that she was a virgin." Then they shall spread the cloth before the city leaders.
18 Then the leaders of that city shall take the man and punish him.
