19 They shall also fine him one hundred pieces of silver and give the money to the young woman's father. The man has given a virgin of Israel a bad name. She shall stay his wife, and he must not divorce her as long as he lives.
20 But if the accusation is true, and there is no proof that the young woman was a virgin,

21 they shall bring her to the door of her father's house. There the men of her city shall stone her to death. She has done a shameful thing in Israel by acting wrongly in her father's house. So you must remove the evil from among you.
22 If a man is found sleeping with another man's wife, both the man and the woman must die. You must remove the evil from Israel.
23 Suppose a virgin is promised in marriage to a man. Another man meets her in the city and sleeps with her.

24 You must take both of them out to the gate of that city and stone them to death. The young woman must die because she did not cry out for help in the city. The man must die because he has wronged his neighbor's wife. So you must remove the evil from among you.
25 But suppose the man meets a promised woman out in the country. He forces her and sleeps with her. Then only the man who did this must die.
26 Do nothing to the young woman. She has done no sin that deserves death. This case is like a man who attacks his neighbor and kills him.
27 The man found her in the field. The promised woman cried out, but no one was there to save her.
28 Suppose a man meets a virgin who is not promised in marriage. He takes hold of her and sleeps with her, and people find out.
29 Then the man who slept with her must pay the young woman's father fifty pieces of silver. She must become his wife because he has wronged her. He must not divorce her as long as he lives.
30 A man must not marry his father's wife. He must not shame his father this way.

title: Deuteronomy 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 No man with hurt or cut private parts may enter the assembly of the LORD.
2 No one born outside of marriage may enter the assembly of the LORD. None of his children may enter, even to the tenth generation.
3 No Ammonite or Moabite may enter the assembly of the LORD. None of their children may enter, even to the tenth generation.
4 They did not bring you food and water on your way out of Egypt. They hired Balaam son of Beor from Pethor in Aram-naharaim to curse you.
5 But the LORD your God would not listen to Balaam. He changed the curse into a blessing for you, because he loves you.
6 Do not seek peace or good things from them, as long as you live.
7 Do not hate an Edomite, because he is your brother. Do not hate an Egyptian, because you lived as a foreigner in his land.
8 The third generation of their children may enter the assembly of the LORD.
9 When you set up camp to fight your enemies, stay away from every bad thing.
10 If a man becomes unclean from a body fluid at night, he must leave the camp and stay outside.
11 When evening comes, he must wash with water. When the sun sets, he may return to the camp.
12 You must have a place outside the camp to go to the toilet.
13 You must also have a tool to dig in your gear. When you go, dig a hole and cover your waste.
14 The LORD your God walks through your camp to protect you. He hands your enemies over to you. So your camp must be holy. He must not see anything unclean among you and turn away.
15 Do not send a servant back to his owner if he has run to you for safety.
16 Let him live among you in any town he likes. Do not treat him badly.
17 No daughter or son of Israel may be a temple prostitute.
18 Do not bring money earned by a prostitute into the house of the LORD your God. Do not use it to keep any promise. The LORD your God hates both of these things.
19 Do not ask your brother for extra money back on a loan. This is true for money, food, or anything else.
20 You may ask a foreigner for extra money back, but not your brother. Then the LORD your God will bless all the work of your hands in the land you are about to take.
21 If you make a promise to the LORD your God, do not wait a long time to keep it. He will surely ask you for it. If you fail, you will be guilty of sin.
22 But if you choose not to make a promise, you will not be guilty of sin.
23 Be careful to do what you said with your mouth. You freely made a promise to the LORD your God.
24 When you enter your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat all the grapes you want. But you must not put any in your basket.
25 When you enter your neighbor's grain field, you may pick the tops of the grain by hand. But you must not use a sickle on his grain.

title: Deuteronomy 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Suppose a man marries a woman, but she is not pleasing to him. He finds something shameful about her. He writes her a letter of divorce, hands it to her, and sends her away.
2 After she leaves his house, she may go and marry another man.
3 Suppose the second man also hates her. He writes her a letter of divorce, hands it to her, and sends her away. Or the second man dies.
4 Then her first husband, who sent her away, must not marry her again. She has become unclean. This is hateful to the LORD. Do not bring sin upon the land the LORD your God is giving you.
5 If a man is newly married, do not send him to war. Do not give him any other job. For one year he is free to stay home and make his wife happy.
6 Do not take grinding stones, or even the upper stone, as a promise for a loan. That would take a person's way to live.
7 If a man is caught stealing one of his Israelite brothers, he must die. He may treat him as a slave or sell him. So you must remove the evil from among you.
8 In cases of bad skin diseases, be careful to follow all that the Levite priests teach you. Do exactly as I have commanded them.
9 Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the way, after you came out of Egypt.
10 When you lend anything to your neighbor, do not go into his house to get his promise item.
11 You must stand outside. The man you are lending to will bring the item out to you.
12 If he is a poor man, do not go to sleep with his item still with you.
13 Be sure to give it back to him by sunset. Then he can sleep in his own coat and bless you. This will be counted as a good and right act before the LORD your God.
14 Do not treat a hired worker badly if he is poor and in need. He may be a brother or a foreigner living in one of your towns.
15 Pay his wages each day before sunset. He is poor and depends on the money. If not, he may cry out to the LORD against you, and you will be guilty of sin.
16 Fathers must not be put to death for their children. Children must not be put to death for their fathers. Each person must die for his own sin.
17 Do not refuse fair treatment to the foreigner or the child with no father. Do not take a widow's coat as a promise for a loan.
18 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt. The LORD your God set you free from that place. So I am commanding you to do this.

19 When you are harvesting in your field and forget a bundle of grain, do not go back to get it. Leave it for the foreigner, the child with no father, and the widow. Then the LORD your God will bless all your work.
20 When you shake the olives down from your trees, do not go over the branches again. What is left is for the foreigner, the child with no father, and the widow.
21 When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, do not go over the vines again. What is left is for the foreigner, the child with no father, and the widow.
22 Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt. So I am commanding you to do this.

title: Deuteronomy 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 When two men have an argument, they must go to court. The judge will set the innocent free and find the guilty one guilty.
2 If the guilty man should be beaten, the judge will make him lie down. He will be beaten in front of the judge. The number of hits will match his crime.
3 He must not get more than forty hits. More than that would shame your brother in your eyes.
4 Do not tie the mouth of an ox while it walks on the grain.
5 When brothers live together and one dies with no son, his widow must not marry outside the family. Her husband's brother must take her as his wife. He must do the duty of a brother-in-law for her.
6 Her first son will carry the name of the dead brother. In this way, his name will not be wiped out from Israel.

7 But the man may not want to marry his brother's widow. Then she will go to the elders at the city gate. She will say, "My husband's brother refuses to keep his brother's name in Israel. He will not do the duty of a brother-in-law for me."
8 Then the elders of his city will call him and speak with him. He may still say, "I do not want to marry her."
9 Then his brother's widow will go up to him in front of the elders. She will take off his sandal and spit in his face. She will say, "This is done to the man who will not keep his brother's line."
10 And his family name in Israel will be called "The House of the Man Without a Sandal."
11 Two men may be fighting. The wife of one may come to help her husband. She may reach out her hand and grab the private parts of the other man.
12 Then you must cut off her hand. You must show her no pity.
13 You must not carry two different weights in your bag, one heavy and one light.
14 You must not keep two different measures in your house, one large and one small.
15 You must use true and honest weights and measures. Then you will live long in the land that the LORD your God gives you.
16 Everyone who cheats in these things is hated by the LORD your God.
17 Remember what the Amalekites did to you on your way from Egypt.
18 They met you on your journey when you were tired and weak. They attacked the weak ones at the back. They had no fear of God.
19 The LORD your God will give you rest from the enemies around you. He will give you the land as a gift to own. Then you must wipe out the memory of Amalek from under heaven. Do not forget!

title: Deuteronomy 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 When you enter the land the LORD your God gives you, take it as your own and live there.
2 Then take some of the first part of all your crops from the soil. Put them in a basket. Go to the place the LORD your God will choose for His Name.
3 Go to the priest who serves at that time. Say to him, "I declare today to the LORD your God that I have come into the land. The LORD swore to our fathers to give it to us."
4 Then the priest will take the basket from your hands. He will place it before the altar of the LORD your God.
5 And you will say before the LORD your God, "**My father was a wandering Aramean**. He went down to Egypt with only a few people. He lived there and became a great nation, strong and many.
6 But the Egyptians treated us badly and made us suffer. They forced us to do hard work.
7 So we cried out to the LORD, the God of our fathers. The LORD heard our cry. He saw our pain, hard work, and trouble.
8 Then the LORD brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand and powerful arm. He came with great fear, signs, and wonders.
9 He brought us to this place. He gave us this land, a land flowing with milk and honey.
10 And now, look, I have brought the first part of the land You, O LORD, have given me." Then place the basket before the LORD your God and bow down before Him.
11 So you will rejoice in all the good things the LORD your God has given you. The Levite and the foreigner living among you will rejoice with your household.
12 In the third year, the year of the tenth, set aside a tenth of all your crops. Give it to the Levite, the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow. They will eat and be filled inside your gates.

13 Then say before the LORD your God, "I have taken the holy share out of my house. I have given it to the Levite, the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow. I have done all You commanded me. I have not broken or forgotten Your commands.

14 I have not eaten any of the holy share while sad for the dead. I have not taken any of it while not clean. I have not offered any of it for the dead. I have obeyed the LORD my God. I have done everything You commanded me.
15 Look down from Your holy home, from heaven. Bless Your people Israel and the land You have given us. You swore this to our fathers — a land flowing with milk and honey."
16 The LORD your God commands you today to follow these rules and laws. You must follow them with all your heart and all your soul.
17 Today you have said that the LORD is your God. You will walk in His ways. You will keep His rules, commands, and laws. You will listen to His voice.
18 And today the LORD has said that you are His people, His special treasure, as He promised. You are to keep all His commands.
19 He will lift you high in praise, name, and honor above all the nations He has made. You will be a holy people to the LORD your God, as He has promised.

title: Deuteronomy 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses and the leaders of Israel spoke to the people: "Keep all the commands I give you today.
2 Soon you will cross the Jordan into the land the LORD your God gives you. On that day, set up large stones and cover them with plaster.
3 Write on them all the words of this law. Do this after you cross over into the land. The LORD your God gives you a land full of milk and honey, just as he promised your fathers.
4 After you cross the Jordan, set up these stones on Mount Ebal. Cover them with plaster, as I command you today.
5 Also build an altar there to the LORD your God. Build it with stones. Do not use any iron tool on them.
6 Build the altar of the LORD your God with stones that no one has cut. Offer burnt offerings on it to the LORD your God.
7 Also offer peace offerings there. Eat them and be glad before the LORD your God.
8 And write all the words of this law clearly on these stones."
9 Then Moses and the priests from the tribe of Levi spoke to all Israel: "Be quiet, O Israel, and listen! Today you have become the people of the LORD your God.
10 So you must obey the voice of the LORD your God. Follow his commands and rules that I give you today."
11 On that day Moses commanded the people:
12 "When you cross the Jordan, these tribes shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.
13 And these tribes shall stand on Mount Ebal to speak the curse: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.
14 Then the Levites shall call out loudly to every Israelite:
15 '**Cursed is** the man who makes a carved idol or metal image. It is a thing the LORD hates. A skilled worker makes it, and the man sets it up in secret.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
16 '**Cursed is** he who does not honor his father or mother.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
17 '**Cursed is** he who moves his neighbor's land marker.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
18 '**Cursed is** he who leads a blind man the wrong way on the road.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
19 '**Cursed is** he who refuses justice to foreigners, orphans, or widows.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
20 '**Cursed is** he who has sex with his father's wife, for he has shamed his father's bed.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
21 '**Cursed is** he who has sex with any animal.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
22 '**Cursed is** he who has sex with his sister, the daughter of his father or his mother.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
23 '**Cursed is** he who has sex with his mother-in-law.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
24 '**Cursed is** he who secretly kills his neighbor.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
25 '**Cursed is** he who takes money to kill an innocent person.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**
26 '**Cursed is** he who does not obey the words of this law.' And **all the people shall say, 'Amen!'**

title: Deuteronomy 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 "If you carefully obey the LORD your God, he will do this. Follow all his commandments I give you today. Then the LORD your God will lift you up above all nations.
2 All these blessings will come and reach you, if you obey the LORD your God:
3 You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the country.
4 Your children will be blessed. Your crops will be blessed. The young of your farm animals will be blessed — the calves and the lambs.
5 Your basket and bread bowl will be blessed.
6 You will be blessed when you come in. You will be blessed when you go out.
7 The LORD will defeat the enemies who rise up against you. They will come at you from one road but run from you on seven roads.
8 The LORD will send a blessing on your barns. He will bless all the work of your hands. The LORD your God will bless you in the land he gives you.
9 The LORD will set you up as his holy people, as he promised. Keep his commandments and walk in his ways.
10 Then all the peoples of the earth will see this. You are called by the name of the LORD. They will stand in fear of you.
11 The LORD will make you grow strong. You will have many children. Your farm animals will increase. Your crops will be rich in the land he promised your fathers.
12 The LORD will open the sky, his great storehouse. He will send rain on your land in the right season. He will bless all the work of your hands. You will lend to many nations but borrow from none.
13 The LORD will make you the head, not the tail. You will only go up and never go down. Listen and carefully follow the commandments of the LORD your God today.
14 Do not turn from any of the words I command you today. Do not turn right or left. Do not go after other gods to serve them.
15 But if you do not obey the LORD your God, this will happen. You must carefully follow all his commandments and rules I give you today. If you do not, all these curses will come and reach you:
16 You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the country.
17 Your basket and bread bowl will be cursed.
18 Your children will be cursed. Your crops will be cursed. The calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks will be cursed.
19 You will be cursed when you come in. You will be cursed when you go out.
20 The LORD will send curses on you. He will send trouble and shame in all you do. You will be destroyed and quickly die. This is because of your evil in leaving him.
21 The LORD will make sickness stick to you. He will wipe you out from the land you are entering to take.
22 The LORD will strike you with wasting sickness, fever, and swelling. He will strike you with burning heat and dry weather. Crop rot and mold will chase you until you die.
23 The sky above you will be like bronze. The earth under you will be like iron.
24 The LORD will turn the rain of your land into dust. It will come down from the sky until you are destroyed.
25 The LORD will let your enemies defeat you. You will come at them from one road but run from them on seven. You will become a thing of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
26 Your dead bodies will be food for all the birds of the sky and the wild animals. No one will scare them away.
27 The LORD will strike you with the sores of Egypt. He will strike you with tumors, scabs, and itch. You will not be cured.
28 The LORD will strike you with madness, blindness, and a confused mind.
29 At noon you will feel your way like a blind man in the dark. You will not succeed in your ways. Day after day people will crush you and steal from you. No one will save you.
30 You will be promised to a woman in marriage. But another man will take her. You will build a house but not live in it. You will plant a vineyard but not enjoy its fruit.
31 Your ox will be killed in front of you. But you will not eat any of it. Your donkey will be taken away and not given back. Your flock will be given to your enemies. No one will save you.
32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another nation. You will watch for them day after day with tired eyes. You will have no power to help.
33 A people you do not know will eat your crops and all your hard work. All your days you will be crushed and broken.
34 The things you see will drive you mad.
35 The LORD will strike you with painful sores that cannot be cured. They will cover your knees and thighs. They will cover you from your feet to your head.
36 The LORD will send you and the king you choose to a strange nation. You and your fathers have not known it. There you will worship other gods made of wood and stone.
37 You will become a thing of horror, mocking, and laughter among all the nations. The LORD will drive you there.
38 You will plant much seed in the field but gather little. The locusts will eat it.
39 You will plant and care for vineyards. But you will not drink the wine or gather the grapes. Worms will eat them.
40 You will have olive trees throughout your land. But you will never anoint yourself with oil. The olives will fall off.
41 You will have sons and daughters, but they will not stay with you. They will be taken away as captives.
42 Swarms of locusts will eat all your trees and your crops.
43 The foreigner among you will rise higher and higher above you. You will sink lower and lower.
44 He will lend to you, but you will not lend to him. He will be the head, and you will be the tail.
45 All these curses will come on you. They will chase you and reach you until you are destroyed. This is because you did not obey the LORD your God or keep his commandments and rules.
46 These curses will be a sign and a warning on you and your children forever.
47 You did not serve the LORD your God with joy and a glad heart in your good times.
48 So you will serve the enemies the LORD will send against you. You will know hunger, thirst, no clothes, and great need. He will put an iron yoke on your neck until he destroys you.
49 The LORD will bring a nation from far away, from the ends of the earth. They will come down on you like an eagle. You will not understand their language.
50 They will be a cruel nation. They will show no respect for the old and no pity for the young.
51 They will eat the young of your farm animals and your crops until you die. They will leave you no grain, new wine, or oil. They will leave no calves or lambs until you are gone.
52 They will surround all your cities throughout the land. The high, strong walls you trust in will fall. They will surround every city in the land the LORD your God has given you.
53 Then you will eat your own children. You will eat the flesh of the sons and daughters the LORD your God gave you. This will happen in the siege your enemy puts on you.
54 The kindest and gentlest man among you will turn cruel. He will turn on his brother, his beloved wife, and his surviving children.
55 He will refuse to share with them the flesh of his children he eats. He has nothing left in the siege your enemy puts on your cities.
56 The kindest and gentlest woman among you will turn cruel. She is so gentle she would never put her foot on the ground. She will turn on her beloved husband, her son, and her daughter.
57 She will secretly eat the baby's afterbirth and the children she gives birth to. She has nothing else in the siege your enemy puts on your cities.
58 You must carefully obey all the words of this law written in this book. You must fear this glorious and awesome name — the LORD your God.
59 If you do not, he will bring strange disasters on you and your children. He will bring serious, lasting plagues and terrible, long sicknesses.
60 He will strike you again with all the diseases you feared in Egypt. They will stick to you.
61 The LORD will also bring on you every sickness and plague not written in this Book of the Law. He will do this until you are destroyed.
62 You were as many as the stars in the sky. But few of you will be left. This is because you did not obey the LORD your God.
63 The LORD was pleased to make you grow strong and many. In the same way, he will be pleased to wipe you out and destroy you. You will be pulled up from the land you are entering to take.

64 Then the LORD will scatter you among all nations. He will scatter you from one end of the earth to the other. There you will worship other gods made of wood and stone. You and your fathers have not known them.
65 Among those nations you will find no rest. There will be no resting place for your foot. There the LORD will give you a shaking heart, failing eyes, and a hopeless soul.
66 Your life will hang in doubt before you. You will be afraid night and day. You will never be sure you will live.
67 In the morning you will say, 'If only it were evening!' In the evening you will say, 'If only it were morning!' This is because of the fear in your heart and the terrible things you will see.
68 The LORD will send you back to Egypt in ships. He said you should never see that road again. There you will try to sell yourselves as slaves to your enemies. But no one will buy you."

title: Deuteronomy 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 These are the words of the covenant. The LORD told Moses to make it with the Israelites in Moab. It was added to the covenant from Horeb.
2 Moses called all Israel together. He said, "You saw with your own eyes everything the LORD did in Egypt.
3 You saw what He did to Pharaoh, his officials, and his whole land. You saw the great signs and wonders.
4 But to this day, the LORD has not given you a mind to understand. He has not given you eyes to see or ears to hear.
5 For forty years I led you in the desert. Your clothes and sandals did not wear out.
6 You ate no bread and drank no wine or strong drink. This was so you would know that I am the LORD your God.
7 When you came to this place, two kings came to fight us. Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan attacked us. But we defeated them.
8 We took their land. We gave it as a gift of land to the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and half of Manasseh.
9 So keep and follow the words of this covenant. Then you will do well in all you do.
10 All of you are standing today before the LORD your God. Your leaders, elders, officials, and all the men of Israel are here.
11 Your children, your wives, and the foreigners in your camps are here too. They cut your wood and draw your water.
12 You are here to enter into the covenant of the LORD your God. He is making this covenant and oath with you today.
13 Today He sets you up as His people. And He will be your God. He promised this to your fathers — Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
14 I am making this covenant and this oath not only with you.
15 I am also making it with those standing here with us today. And with those who are not here today.
16 You yourselves know how we lived in Egypt. You know how we passed through the nations on the way here.
17 You saw their hateful things and idols. They were made of wood, stone, silver, and gold.
18 Make sure no one here turns away from the LORD our God. No man, woman, family, or tribe must go and worship those gods. Make sure no root among you grows poison and bitter fruit.

19 Such a person may hear the words of this oath. He may claim a blessing on himself. He may say, "I will have peace, even if I follow the hardness of my own heart." This will bring trouble on wet land and dry alike.

20 The LORD will never be willing to forgive him. Instead, His anger and jealousy will burn against that man. Every curse in this book will fall on him. The LORD will wipe out his name from under heaven.
21 The LORD will single him out from all the tribes of Israel for disaster. This will follow all the curses of the covenant in this Book of the Law.
22 Then the next generation will see what happened. Your sons and the foreigner from far away will see the plagues and sicknesses.

23 All its soil will be a burning waste of sulfur and salt. Nothing will be planted there. Nothing will grow. It will be like Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim. The LORD destroyed them in His great anger.
24 So all the nations will ask, "Why has the LORD done this to this land? Why this great anger?"
25 And the people will answer, "It is because they left the covenant of the LORD. He is the God of their fathers. He made the covenant when He brought them out of Egypt.
26 They went and served other gods. They worshiped gods they had not known. The LORD had not given them these gods.
27 So the anger of the LORD burned against this land. He brought every curse in this book upon it.
28 The LORD pulled them out of their land in great anger. He threw them into another land, where they are today."
29 **The secret things belong to the LORD our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may follow all the words of this law.**

title: Deuteronomy 30
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 "All these things will come to you — the blessings and the curses I have set before you. You will think about them in all the nations where the LORD your God has sent you away.
2 Then you and your children will return to the LORD your God. You will obey his voice with all your heart and all your soul, just as I command you today.
3 Then he will bring you back from exile. He will show you mercy. He will gather you from all the nations where he scattered you.
4 Even if he has sent you to the farthest place on earth, he will gather you and bring you back.
5 The LORD your God will bring you into the land your fathers owned. You will take it as your own. He will make you do well and grow in number, more than your fathers.
6 The LORD your God will circumcise your hearts and the hearts of your children's children. Then you will love him with all your heart and with all your soul, so that you may live.
7 Then the LORD your God will put all these curses on your enemies who hate you and hurt you.
8 And you will again obey the voice of the LORD. You will follow all his commands that I give you today.

9 So the LORD your God will give you plenty in all you do. He will bless the children you have, the young of your farm animals, and the crops of your land. The LORD will again be glad over you, as he was glad over your fathers.
10 This will happen if you obey the LORD your God and keep his commands and rules written in this Book of the Law. You must turn to him with all your heart and with all your soul.
11 This command I give you today is not too hard for you. It is not too far away.
12 It is not in heaven. You do not need to ask, 'Who will go up to heaven to get it for us and tell it to us, so we may obey it?'
13 And it is not across the sea. You do not need to ask, 'Who will cross the sea to get it for us and tell it to us, so we may obey it?'
14 But the word is very near you. It is in your mouth and in your heart, so that you may obey it.
15 See, I have set before you today life and good things, and also death and terrible loss.

16 Today I command you to love the LORD your God. Walk in his ways. Keep his commands, rules, and laws. Then you will live and grow, and the LORD your God will bless you in the land you are entering to take as your own.
17 But your heart may turn away. You may not listen. You may be drawn away to bow down to other gods and worship them.
18 I tell you today that you will surely die. You will not have a long life in the land you are crossing the Jordan to take as your own.
19 I call heaven and earth to be witnesses against you today. I have set before you life and death, blessings and curses. So choose life, that you and your children's children may live.
20 Love the LORD your God. Obey him. Hold on tight to him. For he is your life. He will give you many years in the land that the LORD promised to give to your fathers — to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob."

title: Deuteronomy 31
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Moses finished speaking all these words to Israel.
2 He said to them, "I am now one hundred and twenty years old. I cannot lead you anymore. The LORD has told me, 'You will not cross the Jordan.'
3 The LORD your God himself will cross over ahead of you. He will destroy these nations before you, and you will take their land. Joshua will cross over ahead of you, as the LORD has said.
4 The LORD will do to them what he did to Sihon and Og. They were the kings of the Amorites, and he destroyed them and their land.
5 The LORD will hand them over to you. You must do to them just as I have commanded you.
6 **Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the LORD your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you.**"

7 Then Moses called Joshua. He said to him in front of all Israel, "Be strong and brave. You will go with this people into the land. The LORD promised it to their fathers. You will give it to them as their own."
8 "The LORD himself goes before you. He will be with you. He will never leave you or forsake you. Do not be afraid or lose hope."
9 So Moses wrote down this law. He gave it to the priests from the family of Levi. They carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD. He also gave it to all the elders of Israel.
10 Then Moses commanded them, "Every seven years, at the time when debts are cancelled, during the Feast of Tabernacles,
11 all Israel will come before the LORD your God at the place he will choose. There you must read this law out loud to all Israel.
12 Gather the people — men, women, children, and the foreigners who live in your towns. They will listen and learn to fear the LORD your God. They will follow all the words of this law.
13 Then their children who do not know the law will listen. They will learn to fear the LORD your God. Do this as long as you live in the land across the Jordan."
14 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Look, the time of your death is near. Call Joshua and come to the Tent of Meeting. There I will give him his orders." So Moses and Joshua came to the Tent of Meeting.
15 Then the LORD appeared at the tent in a tall cloud. The cloud stood over the entrance to the tent.

16 The LORD said to Moses, "Soon you will die and join your ancestors. Then these people will rise up. They will be unfaithful and worship the foreign gods of the land they are entering. They will forsake me and break my covenant with them.

17 On that day my anger will burn against them. I will leave them and turn my face away from them. They will be destroyed. Many troubles and pains will come on them. On that day they will say, 'These disasters have come because our God is no longer with us.'
18 On that day I will surely turn my face away. They have done much evil by turning to other gods.
19 So now, write down this song for yourselves. Teach it to the people of Israel. Make them sing it, so that it will be a witness for me against them.

20 I will bring them into the land I promised to their fathers. It is a rich land full of milk and honey. They will eat well and become rich. Then they will turn to other gods and worship them. They will reject me and break my covenant.
21 When many troubles and pains come on them, this song will speak against them. Their children will not forget it. I know the way they think, even before I bring them into the land I promised."
22 That same day Moses wrote down this song. He taught it to the people of Israel.
23 Then the LORD gave orders to Joshua son of Nun. He said, "Be strong and brave. You will bring the people of Israel into the land I promised them. I will be with you."
24 Moses finished writing the words of this law in a book, from beginning to end.
25 Then he gave this command to the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
26 "Take this Book of the Law. Place it beside the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God. It will stay there as a witness against you.
27 I know how rebellious and stubborn you are. You are already rebelling against the LORD while I am still alive. How much more will you rebel after I die!
28 Gather before me all the elders of your tribes and all your officers. I will speak these words to them. I will call the sky and the earth as witnesses against them.

29 I know that after I die you will become very evil. You will turn away from the path I commanded you. In the days to come, disaster will come on you. You will do evil in the sight of the LORD and make him angry by what your hands do."
30 Then Moses spoke aloud to all the people of Israel. He recited the words of this song from beginning to end.

title: Deuteronomy 32
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Listen, you heavens, and I will speak; hear, you earth, the words of my mouth.
2 Let my teaching fall like rain. Let my words settle like dew. Let them be like soft rain on new grass, like showers on young plants.
3 For I will tell out the name of the LORD. Give greatness to our God!
4 He is the Rock; his works are perfect, and all his ways are right. He is a faithful God who does no wrong. He is upright and just.
5 His people have acted wrongly against him. The fault is not his; they are not his true children. They are a twisted and bent generation.
6 Is this how you repay the LORD, you foolish people with no sense? Is he not your Father, your Creator? Did he not make you and form you?
7 Remember the old days. Think about the long years that have passed. Ask your father, and he will tell you. Ask your elders, and they will teach you.
8 When the Most High gave the nations their lands, when he divided the sons of man, he set the borders of the peoples by the number of the sons of God.
9 For the LORD's share is his people. Jacob is the land he chose for himself.
10 He found him in a desert land, in an empty, howling wild place. He went around him and taught him. He guarded him like the pupil of his eye.
11 Like an eagle that wakes up its nest and hovers over its young, he spread his wings to catch them. He carried them on his strong wings.
12 The LORD alone led him. No foreign god was with him.
13 He made him ride on the high places of the land. He fed him with the fruit of the fields. He gave him honey from the rock and oil from the hard stone,
14 with cream from the herd and milk from the flock, with fat lambs, with rams from Bashan, and goats. He gave him the best wheat. He gave him wine from the finest grapes.
15 But Jeshurun grew fat and kicked back. He became fat, bloated, and full. He left the God who made him. He despised the Rock who saved him.
16 They made him jealous with foreign gods. They made him angry with disgusting things.
17 They gave offerings to demons, not to God. They gave them to gods they had not known, new gods that their fathers did not fear.
18 You ignored the Rock who brought you forth. You forgot the God who gave you birth.
19 When the LORD saw this, he turned away from them. His sons and daughters had made him angry.
20 He said, "I will hide my face from them. I will see what will happen to them in the end. For they are a twisted generation, children who are not faithful.

21 They made me jealous with what is not God. They made me angry with their worthless idols. So I will make them jealous with a people who are not a people. I will make them angry with a nation that has no understanding.
22 For a fire has been lit by my anger. It burns down to the depths of Sheol. It eats up the earth and its harvest. It scorches the roots of the mountains.
23 I will pile troubles on them. I will spend all my arrows against them.
24 They will be wasted by hunger and worn down by deadly sickness. I will send wild animals with sharp teeth against them, with the poison of snakes that move in the dust.
25 Outside, the sword will take their children. Inside, terror will strike the young man and the young woman, the baby and the gray-haired old man.
26 I would have said, 'I will cut them to pieces. I will wipe their name from people's minds,'
27 if I had not feared the mocking of the enemy. Their foes might misunderstand and say, 'Our own hand has won. The LORD did not do all this.'"
28 Israel is a nation with no good advice. They have no understanding among them.
29 If only they were wise, they would understand this. They would see how it will end for them.
30 How could one man chase a thousand? How could two make ten thousand run away? Only if their Rock had sold them. Only if the LORD had handed them over.
31 For their rock is not like our Rock. Even our enemies admit this.
32 Their vine grows from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are poisonous. Their bunches are bitter.
33 Their wine is the poison of snakes, the deadly venom of cobras.
34 "Have I not stored these things up? Have I not sealed them in my vaults?
35 Vengeance is mine; I will pay them back. In time, their foot will slip. Their day of trouble is near. Their doom is coming quickly."
36 For the LORD will defend his people. He will have pity on his servants. He will see that their strength is gone, and no one is left, slave or free.
37 He will say, "Where are their gods? Where is the rock they ran to for safety?
38 Those gods ate the fat of their offerings. They drank the wine of their drink offerings. Let them rise up and help you! Let them give you shelter!
39 See now that I myself am he! There is no god besides me. I put to death and I bring to life. I have wounded and I will heal. No one can save anyone out of my hand.
40 For I lift my hand to heaven and say, 'As surely as I live forever,
41 when I sharpen my flashing sword, and my hand takes hold of it in judgment, I will take vengeance on my foes. I will pay back those who hate me.
42 I will make my arrows drunk with blood. My sword will eat up flesh — the blood of the killed and the captives, the heads of the enemy leaders.'"

43 Rejoice, you heavens, with him! Let all God's angels worship him! Rejoice, you nations, with his people! For he will avenge the blood of his servants. He will take vengeance on his foes. He will pay back those who hate him. He will cleanse his land and his people.
44 Then Moses came with Joshua son of Nun. They spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.
45 When Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel,
46 he said to them, "Take to heart all these words I have testified to you today. Then you can command your children to follow all the words of this law carefully.
47 For they are not empty words for you. They are your life. By them you will live long in the land. You are crossing the Jordan to take that land."
48 On that same day the LORD said to Moses,
49 "Go up into the Abarim hills, to Mount Nebo. It is in the land of Moab, across from Jericho. Look at the land of Canaan. I am giving it to the Israelites as their own.
50 There on the mountain that you climb, you will die. You will be gathered to your people. Your brother Aaron died the same way on Mount Hor.
51 For at the waters of Meribah-kadesh, in the Wilderness of Zin, both of you were not faithful to me before the Israelites. You did not honor me as holy among them.
52 You shall see the land from a distance. But you shall not enter the land I am giving the Israelites."

title: Deuteronomy 33
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 This is the blessing that Moses, the man of God, spoke over Israel before he died.
2 He said: "The LORD came from Sinai. He rose like the morning from Seir. He shone out from Mount Paran. He came with ten thousands of holy ones. Burning fire was in his right hand.
3 Truly you love the people. All the holy ones are in your hand. They sit down at your feet. Each one receives your words —
4 the law that Moses gave us, the gift for the people of Jacob.
5 So the LORD became King over Jeshurun. The leaders of the people gathered. The tribes of Israel came together.
6 Let Reuben live and not die. Let his men not be few."
7 And about Judah he said: "O LORD, hear the cry of Judah. Bring him to his people. With his own hands he defends his cause. Be his help against his enemies."
8 About Levi he said: "Give your Thummim and Urim to Levi, your faithful one. You tested him at Massah. You fought with him at the waters of Meribah.
9 He said of his father and mother, 'I do not regard them.' He did not favor his brothers. He did not know his own sons. For he kept your word and held to your covenant.
10 He will teach your rules to Jacob and your law to Israel. He will put incense before you and whole burnt gifts on your altar.
11 Bless all that he owns, O LORD. Accept the work of his hands. Crush the strength of those who attack him. Crush his enemies so they cannot rise again."
12 About Benjamin he said: "The one the LORD loves rests safe in him. God protects him all day long. He rests on the LORD's shoulders."
13 About Joseph he said: "May the LORD bless his land with the sweet dew from the sky above, and the deep waters that lie below,
14 with the rich harvest from the sun, and the rich crops of every season,
15 with the best of the old mountains, and the riches of the lasting hills,
16 with the best gifts of the land and all that is in it, and with the favor of God who lived in the burning bush. May all these rest on the head of Joseph, the leader among his brothers.

17 He has the strength of a firstborn bull. His horns are like a wild ox's horns. With them he will push back the nations to the ends of the earth. These are the ten thousands of Ephraim, and the thousands of Manasseh."
18 About Zebulun he said: "Be glad, Zebulun, when you go out. And you, Issachar, be glad in your tents.
19 They will call the peoples to a mountain. There they will offer right sacrifices. For they will enjoy the riches of the seas, and the hidden treasures in the sand."
20 About Gad he said: "Blessed is the one who gives more land to Gad! Gad lies down like a lion. He tears off an arm or a head.
21 He chose the best land for himself. The leader's share was kept for him there. He came with the leaders of the people. He carried out the LORD's justice and rules for Israel."
22 About Dan he said: "Dan is a young lion that jumps out of Bashan."
23 About Naphtali he said: "Naphtali is full of favor, full of the blessing of the LORD. He will take the sea and the south as his own."
24 And about Asher he said: "May Asher be the most blessed son of all. May he be the most favored among his brothers. May he dip his foot in oil.
25 May the locks on your gate be iron and bronze. May your strength last all your days."
26 "There is no one like the God of Jeshurun, who rides across the sky to help you. He rides the clouds in his glory.
27 The eternal God is your home. Underneath you are the everlasting arms. He pushes out the enemy in front of you. He says, 'Destroy him!'
28 So Israel lives safely. Jacob's fountain stays safe in a land of grain and new wine, where the sky drops dew.

29 You are blessed, O Israel! Who is like you? You are a people saved by the LORD. He is your shield that protects you, your sword that you trust. Your enemies will bow down in fear before you. You will walk over their high places."

title: Deuteronomy 34
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Deuteronomy
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses climbed Mount Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, which faces Jericho. And the LORD showed him the whole land — from Gilead to Dan,
2 all of Naphtali, the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, all the land of Judah as far as the Western Sea,
3 the Negev, and the region from the Valley of Jericho (the City of Palms) all the way to Zoar.

4 The LORD said to him, "This is the land I promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I said, 'I will give it to your children's children.' I have let you see it with your own eyes. But you will not cross into it."
5 So Moses the servant of the LORD died there in the land of Moab, as the LORD had said.
6 The LORD buried him in a valley in Moab, facing Beth-peor. No one to this day knows where his grave is.
7 Moses died at the age of 120, yet his eyes were not weak nor his strength gone.
8 The Israelites cried for Moses on the plains of Moab for thirty days. Then the time of crying and sadness came to an end.
9 Now Joshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom. This was because Moses had put his hands on him. So the Israelites obeyed him. They did what the LORD had commanded Moses.
10 Since that time, no prophet has risen in Israel like Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face —
11 no prophet who did all the signs and wonders that the LORD sent Moses to do. He did them in Egypt, to Pharaoh, to all his leaders, and to all his land.
12 And no prophet did the great and mighty works that Moses did in front of all Israel.
